articles.title: Cisco Unified CM flaw CVE-2026-20230 now exploited in attacks
articles.description: A high-severity SSRF vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20230, in Cisco Unified Communications Manager Server is now being exploited in attacks. [...]

articles.title: Tata Electronics confirms cyberattack as hackers leak data
articles.description: Tata Electronics has confirmed in a statement to BleepingComputer that it was the target of a cyberattack that impacted parts of its IT infrastructure. [...]

articles.title: Healthtech firm Xolis suffers data breach impacting 1.4 million people
articles.description: Healthcare technology company Xsolis says that sensitive data belonging to nearly 1.4 million individuals was compromised in a phishing attack that gave attackers access to its network. [...]

articles.title: New macOS ClickFix attack silently mounts DMGs to push infostealer
articles.description: A new macOS ClickFix campaign is using Terminal commands to silently download, mount, and launch info-stealing malware from malicious disk image (DMG) files. [...]

articles.title: Scattered Spider members plead guilty to hacking Transport for London
articles.description: Two members of the 'Scattered Spider' cybercrime group pleaded guilty to hacking the Transport for London (TfL) systems in 2024. [...]

articles.title: The Exploit Doesn't Exist. You Can Still Prove It Works Against You
articles.description: Attackers can now weaponize newly disclosed vulnerabilities far faster than most organizations can patch them. Picus Security explains how security teams can validate exploitability before a public exploit even exists. [...]

articles.title: LastPass confirms data breach in Klue supply chain attack
articles.description: LastPass announced that hackers accessed customer data from its Salesforce environment after stealing the company's OAuth tokens in the Klue supply chain attack earlier this month. [...]

articles.title: Webinar: Why email security teams are drowning in alerts
articles.description: Phishing, BEC, and account takeover attacks continue to overwhelm security teams with alerts and investigations. This webinar explores how behavioral AI can help automate detection and response workflows, reducing alert fatigue and improving operational efficiency. [...]

articles.title: WhatsApp phishing attack uses fake business docs to hack PCs
articles.description: An ongoing malware campaign is targeting WhatsApp users in multiple countries with deceptive messages that push VBScript files, leading to remote system access. [...]

articles.title: JaredFromSubway MEV bot hacked in $15 million crypto theft
articles.description: The JaredFromSubway Ethereum MEV (Maximal Extractable Value) bot suffered a $15 million loss after an attacker manipulated the opportunity-detection logic by creating fake cryptocurrency trading opportunities. [...]

articles.title: FFmpeg fixes PixelSmash flaw in widely used video decoder
articles.description: A newly disclosed FFmpeg flaw dubbed 'PixelSmash' could be exploited for remote code execution on Jellyfin servers under certain conditions, and can also trigger a denial-of-service  condition in applications like Kodi, Emby, Nextcloud, PhotoPrism, and OBS Studio. [...]

articles.title: FortiBleed campaign used custom FortiGate sniffer to steal credentials
articles.description: Security firm SOCRadar says the large-scale FortiBleed campaign targeting Fortinet FortiGate devices used custom sniffers to harvest authentication secrets from compromised firewalls and steal credentials. [...]

articles.title: Microsoft fixes AutoGen Studio flaw that enabled code execution
articles.description: A vulnerability chain dubbed AutoJack in Microsoft's AutoGen Studio interface for prototyping AI agents could let attackers manipulate an agent into executing arbitrary commands on its host system simply by visiting a malicious webpage. [...]

articles.title: A Glimpse into the “Search Your Target” Market for Stolen Credentials
articles.description: Attackers no longer need to sift through massive credential dumps. They can pay others to do it for them. Flare explores how an emerging underground market searches stolen credential databases for specific companies, domains, and accounts. [...]

articles.title: AryStinger botnet infected thousands of D-Link routers worldwide
articles.description: A previously undocumented malware botnet named AryStinger has compromised more than 4,000 outdated routers to turn them into proxies for malicious traffic. [...]

articles.title: New Prinz Eugen ransomware prioritizes recent files for encryption
articles.description: A new ransomware operation named 'Prinz Eugen' prioritizes recently modified files for encryption and leaves no ransom note on the system. [...]

articles.title: Microsoft links Mastra AI supply chain attack to North Korean hackers
articles.description: Microsoft has attributed a recent Mastra AI supply chain attack that compromised more than 140 npm packages to the North Korean hacking group Sapphire Sleet, also known as BlueNoroff. [...]

articles.title: Klue OAuth breach victim list grows as Icarus hackers claim attack
articles.description: Market intelligence platform Klue has publicly confirmed a recent security incident that allowed threat actors to steal OAuth tokens used to connect to customers' Salesforce environments, as the new "Icarus" extortion group publicly claims the attack. [...]

articles.title: Hackers exploit info disclosure bug in Gravity SMTP WordPress plugin
articles.description: Threat actors are exploiting an unauthenticated information disclosure vulnerability in the WordPress plugin Gravity SMTP, active on 100,000 sites. [...]

articles.title: Texas govt data breach exposes over 3 million driver’s licenses
articles.description: The Texas Parks and Wildlife Department (TPWD) disclosed a data breach at its license system vendor that exposed personal information for more than three million individuals. [...]

articles.title: Every AI Agent Is an Identity. Most Organizations Don't Treat Them That Way
articles.description: AI agents can access data, trigger workflows, deploy code, and interact with critical business systems, often with little oversight. Token Security breaks down why AI agents are becoming a new identity and governance challenge. [...]

articles.title: Webinar: How attackers bypass MFA and how defenders can respond
articles.description: Modern phishing attacks, including Device Code phishing, can undermine MFA protections and grant attackers access to corporate accounts without stealing passwords. This webinar explores how behavioral AI can help security teams detect compromised accounts faster and automate response workflows. [...]

articles.title: CISA: Splunk Enterprise flaw actively exploited, patch by Sunday
articles.description: CISA has urged U.S. federal agencies to secure their systems by Sunday against a critical Splunk Enterprise vulnerability that is being exploited in attacks. [...]

articles.title: NY man charged after harassing college student with AI-generated nudes
articles.description: A New York man faces cyberstalking charges after allegedly sharing AI-generated nude images and fabricated racist messages using fake social media profiles to harass a Georgia college student. [...]

articles.title: CISA warns Fortinet users to secure devices after FortiBleed leak
articles.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) urged Fortinet customers to secure their devices after nearly 74,000 firewall and VPN credentials were exposed in a data leak dubbed "FortiBleed." [...]

articles.title: Gentlemen ransomware uses multiple EDR killers to disable defenses
articles.description: The Gentlemen ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) is actively developing and maintaining a suite of endpoint detection and response (EDR) killers to help affiliates evade detection in attacks. [...]

articles.title: Nintendo confirms data stolen in WebMD subsidiary cyberattack
articles.description: Nintendo of America has confirmed to BleepingComputer that threat actors stole survey data from the third-party TinyPulse service used internally, but its systems were not compromised. [...]

articles.title: USB worm spreads crypto-stealing malware via Windows shortcut files
articles.description: Threat actors targeting cryptocurrency wallets have been distributing clipboard-stealing malware with self-spreading capabilities and using the Tor network to conceal communication. [...]

articles.title: Klue OAuth breach linked to 'Icarus' Salesforce data theft attacks
articles.description: Market intelligence platform Klue suffered a OAuth breach that enabled the "Icarus" threat actors to steal Salesforce CRM data from multiple organizations in an ongoing extortion campaign. [...]

articles.title: 5 reasons Microsoft 365 backup isn’t enough for business data protection
articles.description: Microsoft 365 helps keep services running, but protecting and recovering business data remains your responsibility. Acronis breaks down five gaps organizations should consider when evaluating Microsoft 365 data protection. [...]

articles.title: Police cleans nearly 15,000 SocGholish-infected sites tied to Evil Corp
articles.description: International law enforcement agencies cleaned nearly 15,000 malware-infected WordPress websites and took down more than 100 servers linked to the SocGholish botnet and the Evil Corp Russian cybercrime group. [...]
